earnings_analysis.details: EPS was positive in all available periods.; EPS did not grow from earliest to latest period.

strength_analysis.details: Current ratio = 1.92 (moderately strong).; Debt ratio = 0.32, under 0.50 (conservative).; Company did not pay dividends in these periods.

valuation_analysis.details: NCAV not exceeding market cap or insufficient data for net-net approach.; Graham Number = 67.80; Margin of Safety (Graham Number) = -82.15%; Price close to or above Graham Number, low margin of safety.

macro_context.market_sentiment: Strongly bullish to start May 2026: S&P 500 hit a fresh all-time intraday high (+0.54%), Nasdaq +0.69%, Russell 2000 +2.21%, driven by Apple earnings beat and Middle East peace hopes reducing oil risk premium. April was the best monthly performance for both the S&P 500 and Nasdaq since 2020.
macro_context.fed_outlook: FOMC voted 8-4 on April 29 to hold rates steady at 3.5%-3.75%; unusual four-member dissent signals internal disagreement. One dissenter (Miran) wanted an immediate 25bp cut, while three others opposed any easing bias in the statement. Elevated energy-driven inflation limits near-term cut probability. Powell remains on the Fed board indefinitely despite Trump legal pressure.
macro_context.geopolitical: Middle East conflict (Strait of Hormuz disruption risk, oil supply damage) remains the primary macro flashpoint; IMF projects 19% energy price rise and global growth of only 3.1% for 2026. Additional risks: U.S.-Venezuela tensions, NATO-Greenland posturing, Iran civil unrest, and ongoing tariff uncertainty. On May 1, a response through Pakistani mediators to a U.S. draft peace proposal eased the near-term risk premium and pulled oil lower.

ticker_news.headline: Alphabet Q1 2026: Revenue $109.9B (+20% YoY), net income $62.58B (+81% YoY), EPS $5.11 — massive beat vs. $2.62 consensus
Google Cloud revenue surpasses $20B for first time, growing 63% YoY; cloud backlog nearly doubled QoQ to over $460B
Alphabet raises full-year 2026 capex to $180B-$190B and signals 2027 capex will 'significantly increase' for AI infrastructure
GOOG shares surged ~10% post-earnings on April 29-30; six Wall Street firms raised price targets after cloud growth acceleration
Google I/O 2026 scheduled May 19-20 with expected major AI and product announcements; Alphabet reportedly considering up to $40B investment in Anthropic

analyst_consensus.recent_changes: Consensus upgraded to Strong Buy across major platforms (37-68 analysts). Raymond James raised PT to $400 (Jan 22, 2026); Wells Fargo raised PT to $387 and upgraded from Equal-Weight (Feb 23, 2026). Six additional firms raised PTs on April 30 following Q1 beat. Avg PT ~$357-$385 range depending on source; median $375. PT revised upward ~13.71% over the past 3 months. One Seeking Alpha downgrade citing valuation concerns, but sell-side is overwhelmingly bullish. Source: MarketBeat, Benzinga, StockAnalysis.

web_context.competitor_activity: Microsoft announced continued heavy Azure capex but remains capacity-constrained through 2026; stock was roughly flat post-earnings. Meta raised 2026 capex guidance to $125B-$145B (+$10B) and fell ~9% on profitability concerns. Amazon set a record $200B 2026 capex for AWS data centers and rose ~2.7% post-earnings. Alphabet outperformed all three rivals in post-earnings market reaction (+10%), with Fortune noting it was the only hyperscaler to convince investors that AI spending is already paying off. Alphabet is reportedly exploring a $40B investment in Anthropic, a direct challenge to Microsoft's OpenAI partnership. Source: Fortune (https://fortune.com/2026/04/29/microsoft-meta-google-ai-capex-spending-billions/), Invezz.
web_context.earnings_info: Q1 2026 reported April 29, 2026 — beat on all metrics (EPS $5.11 vs. $2.62 estimate, revenue $109.9B vs. $107.2B estimate). Next earnings: Q2 2026 expected around July 22, 2026 (unconfirmed; some sources say July 23). Q2 EPS analyst estimate: $2.79; Q2 Revenue estimate: ~$110.71B. No earnings-related blackout risk for at least 80 days. Source: Nasdaq earnings calendar, MarketBeat.
